title: Process Execution and IPC Following an exec() Call
description: A flowchart showing how a process uses fork(), exec(), and different IPC methods to create and communicate with a new program.
elements: IPC Methods (Pipes, Shared Memory, Message Queues)
relationships: Parent calls fork() to create a child copy
Child calls exec() to replace its memory space with a new program
The original parent and the new program must use established IPC methods to communicate, as shared state from the fork is lost after exec
